narration: Your rewards card is paying you back — but that's not the whole story. Studies show people spend 23% more with rewards cards than with cash. Wild, right?
visual: Dark background. Large credit card SVG with a golden star badge. Coins arc upward toward the card. Text: 23% MORE animates in bold green.

narration: MIT ran a real study where people paid double for the same item when using a card versus cash. The reward psychology literally shuts off your spending pain.
visual: Light background. Two price tags side by side: cash tag shows $21, card tag shows $42. Dollar bill icon above left, credit card icon above right. MIT STUDY label.

narration: The average rewards user earns about $167 in cashback per year. But that 23% extra spending adds up to over $2,400 in purchases you wouldn't have made otherwise.
visual: Dark background. A balance scale SVG. Left pan holds small coin stack labeled $167 EARNED. Right pan holds tall money stack labeled $2,400 EXTRA — scale tips hard right.

narration: Here's why: every points notification is a tiny dopamine hit. Your brain starts chasing rewards instead of tracking what you're spending — and that's the trap.
visual: Light background. Phone SVG with notification popup showing a star and 'You earned 50 pts!'. Circular arrow loop: SPEND → REWARD → SPEND. Brain icon pulsing.

narration: The fix isn't cutting your card. Use it only for fixed bills — rent, utilities, subscriptions. Never for groceries or impulse shopping. Capture rewards without the behavior trigger.
visual: Dark background. Checklist: RENT ✓, ELECTRIC ✓, INTERNET ✓ — each in green. Then GROCERIES and SHOPPING with red X marks. Card icon locked to the checked items only.

narration: Card companies made $189 billion in interchange fees last year. They can afford your 2% cashback — follow for the money tricks they hope you never find out.
visual: Light background. Bank building SVG with arrows showing money flowing IN. Large text: $189 BILLION. Small cashback coin flying back labeled 2%. Follow CTA at bottom.
